What Is Saturn (Shani) in Vedic Astrology?

Shani (Saturn) governs discipline, karma, longevity, hardship in Vedic astrology. Its gem is Blue Sapphire.

Saturn (Shani) in Vedic Astrology Explained

In Vedic astrology, the positioning of Saturn—known as Shani—offers a structured lens for understanding life’s cycles. While Western systems often frame Saturn as a marker of boundaries, the saturn shani vedic framework emphasizes how planetary movements intersect with personal responsibility and sustained growth. Many find that viewing these transit patterns symbolically invites clearer perspective rather than fixed outcomes.
